<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> God is just below the surface and details of our lives, not causing them, but relating to all of them and especially to us.<|12.88|>
<|14.40|> Sometimes God is far from subtle and only too clear.<|19.10|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> giving us a realization or an epiphany that if we have the courage to really feel it and be honest about it,<|7.42|>
<|7.72|> it could change the entire course of the rest of our lives.<|11.96|>
<|13.24|> Sometimes in the present moment, we can't see or feel or sense God at all.<|18.80|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But years later, even decades later, in retrospect, we can look back and see the contours of God's presence tracing our footsteps.<|10.40|>
<|12.62|> Whether God is subtle in your life or all too clear,<|18.80|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Whether we are conscious of God's presence or not,<|5.26|>
<|6.32|> the point seems to be, thankfully,<|8.98|>
<|9.94|> that God's spirit is always, always present.<|15.02|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.00|><|endoftext|>
